LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ROBERT FERRIS AND ROBERT BOCKELMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS CONTINUED THE MATTER TO AUGUST 12, 2008. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ROBERT FERRIS AND ROBERT BOCKELMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S INABILITY TO PRESENT EXPERT TESTIMONY IS A NEW FACT AND DEFENDANT HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN BY DEMONSTRATING THAT EXPERT TESTIMONY IS NECESSARY TO PROVE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. EXPERT TESTIMONY IS NECESSARY TO PROVE THE PROPERTY IS SUBJECT TO THE RENT CONTROL ORDINANCE AND TO EXPLAIN WHY PLAINTIFF DID NOT WAIVE THE EVICTION CLAIM WHEN SIGNING THE STIPULATION AND JUDGMENT. THESE ISSUES MUST BE ADDRESSED BEFORE PLAINTIFF CAN ASSERT THAT DEFENDANT GAVE ERRONEOUS ADVICE REGARDING THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A STAY OF THE RULING PENDING A MOTION IN DISCOVERY IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WERE SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS DEBRA LEMMONS, JEMEESE LEMMONS, AND JESSICA LEMMONS MOTION TO SET ASIDE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE ISSUES OF LEGAL MALPRACTICE PRESENTED BY THIS CASE REQUIRED EXPERT TESTIMONY, PARTICULARLY GIVEN THE DISPUTE OVER THE APPLICABILITY OF THE RENT ORDINANCE. THE NEED FOR SUCH TESTIMONY IS WITHIN THE PURVIEW OF A LAWYER'S EXPERTISE. THIS IS NOT A MISTAKE CHARGEABLE TO A REASONABLY PRUDENT PERSON LACKING LEGAL TRAINING. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ROBERT FERRIS, ROBERT BOCKELMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. GIVEN THE COURT'S RULINGS ON PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS, DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. IN THE ALTERNATIVE, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS REFLECTED IN FACTS EIGHT AND EIGHTEEN. AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD ISSUES, DENIED BECAUSE A FAVORABLE RULINGS WOULD NOT RESOLVE THE ENTIRE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION ONE IS SUSTAINED. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE CANNOT BE USED TO ESTABLISH THE TRUTH OF THE CONTENTS OF THE DOCUMENT. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TWO AND THREE ARE SUSTAINED AS LEGAL CONCLUSIONS. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO FOUR IS SUSTAINED AS IT HAS NOT BEEN PROPERLY AUTHENTICATED. MR. BRACAMONTES IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER ROBERT BALIAN, CSR 5220

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-10-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS DEBRA LEMMONS, JEMEESE LEMMONS, AND JESSICA LEMMONS MOTION TO SET ASIDE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. THE ISSUES OF LEGAL MALPRACTICE PRESENTED BY THIS CASE REQUIRED EXPERT TESTIMONY, PARTICULARLY GIVEN THE DISPUTE OVER THE APPLICABILITY OF THE RENT ORDINANCE. THE NEED FOR SUCH TESTIMONY IS WITHIN THE PURVIEW OF A LAWYER'S EXPERTISE. THIS IS NOT A MISTAKE CHARGEABLE TO A REASONABLY PRUDENT PERSON LACKING LEGAL TRAINING. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Ferris (626.6975) for defendants and moving parties
Michael Bracamontes of Bradshaw & Associates (433.4800) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS ROBERT FERRIS AND ROBERT BOCKELMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is granted. Plaintiff's inability to present expert testimony is a new fact and defendant has shifted the burden by demonstrating that expert testimony is necessary to prove plaintiff's claim. Expert testimony is necessary to prove the property is subject to the Rent Control Ordinance and to explain why plaintiff did not waive the eviction claim when signing the stipulation and judgment. These issues must be addressed before plaintiff can assert that defendant gave erroneous advice regarding the statute of limitations. Plaintiff's evidentiary objections are overruled. Plaintiff's request for a stay of the ruling pending a motion in Discovery is denied. Order signed in open Court. Plaintiff's evidentiary objections were signed in open Court. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ROBERT FERRIS, ROBERT BOCKELMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: MICHAEL R. BRACAMONTES, ESQUIRE (BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, 415.433.4800) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. ROBERT L. FERRIS, ESQUIRE (415.626.6975) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS FERRIS & BOCKELMAN. ANNEMARIE MCDOWELL, ESQUIRE (LOW BALL & LYNCH, 415.981.6630) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS JACKIE ROBERTSON GARDEN APARTMENTS AND JOHN STEWART CO.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. GIVEN THE COURT'S RULINGS ON PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS, DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. IN THE ALTERNATIVE, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS REFLECTED IN FACTS EIGHT AND EIGHTEEN. AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD ISSUES, DENIED BECAUSE A FAVORABLE RULINGS WOULD NOT RESOLVE THE ENTIRE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION ONE IS SUSTAINED. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE CANNOT BE USED TO ESTABLISH THE TRUTH OF THE CONTENTS OF THE DOCUMENT. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TWO AND THREE ARE SUSTAINED AS LEGAL CONCLUSIONS. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO FOUR IS SUSTAINED AS IT HAS NOT BEEN PROPERLY AUTHENTICATED. MR. BRACAMONTES IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER ROBERT BALIAN, CSR 5220

APPEARANCE:
Michael R. Bracamontes, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Debra Lemmons
Robert J. Bockelman, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Robert Ferris and Robert J. Bockelman
AnneMarie McDowell, Esq., for defendant Jackie Robinson Garden Apartments

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Debra Lemmons' motion to compel responses to interrogatories is moot as supplemental responses have been served. The Court makes no rulings as to the sufficiency of the answers. The Court finds that defendant failed to respond in a timely fashion thereby requiring the filing of the instant motion. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $445 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Testimony And For Monetary Sanctions;
Off Calendar Sep-07-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Robert J. Bockelman, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Robert Ferris and Robert Bockelman
Michael R. Bracamontes, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Debra Lemmons
AnneMarie McDowell, Esq., for third party: Jackie Robinson Garden Appartments

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendants Bockelman and Ferris' motion to compel deposition testimony is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to complete the deposition by permitting defendant Bockelman the opportunity to conduct his examination at a time mutually convenient to the parties. The deposition shall be completed within 30 days of notice of this order. If there are any further depositions in the future, the parties are ordered to clearly state their appearances to avoid any ambiguity. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
